notes: Cornelia, mother of the Gracchi brothers, noted for her noble and chaste character and her dignified mourning of her sons' deaths.

docs.title: Life of Caius Gracchus
docs.author: Plutarch
docs.excerpt: … bore the loss of her two sons with a noble and undaunted spirit, so, in reference to the holy places in which they were slain, she said, their dead bodies were well worthy of such sepulchres.

docs.title: Life of Tiberius Gracchus
docs.author: Plutarch
docs.excerpt: … her children, approved herself so discreet a matron, so affectionate a mother, and so constant and noble-spirited a widow, that Tiberius seemed to all men to have done nothing unreasonable, in choosing to die for such a woman; who, when king Ptolemy himself proffered her his crown, and
